items.title: An emergency permit of mouse bait has been approved by regulator

children.content: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.

items.title: Chinese-backed investors ordered to sell holdings in crucial WA rare earths company

image.altText: A sign explaining a new mine sits in red dirt as a person walks past in boots

children.content: The federal treasurer orders another six companies to divest their holdings in rare earths miner Northern Minerals as concerns grow in Canberra that Chinese-backed investors are trying to seize control of the company.

items.title: 'Bro, there's a frog in my lettuce': Woolworths investigates salad bag find

children.content: A Kiwi couple in the town of Esperance on WA's south coast share an unlikely discovery, a live frog in a bag of Woolworths lettuce.

image.altText: A small frog in a bag of lettuce

gallery.altText: A frog inside a plastic bag with lettuce on a table
A green frog sitting on leaves

thumbnail.altText: A frog inside a plastic bag with lettuce on a table
A green frog sitting on leaves

items.title: Bid to reopen Albany hydrotherapy pool rejected

children.content: The WA health minister rejects recommissioning Albany's public hydrotherapy pool despite a feasibility study saying there is a justifiable cause to do so.

image.altText: Older women standing in her lush garden using a walking frame

items.title: Six shops ordered to shut under new illegal tobacco laws

children.content: More than 140,000 illegal cigarettes have been seized and six stores ordered to close down, days after Western Australia's new tobacco laws came into force.

image.altText: A shop front with 'store closed' banners stuck on the front windows

items.title: 'Devoted father and gentleman': Tributes flow for shark attack victim

children.content: The wife of 38-year-old Perth man Steven Mattaboni says her family's "hearts are irrevocably broken" after he was killed in a shark attack while spearfishing off the coast of Rottnest Island on Saturday.

articles.title: Friends witness 'horrific' shark attack that claimed life of spearfishing mate

children.content: A 38-year-old man had been spearfishing with friends when he was killed in a shark attack off Rottnest Island on Saturday morning.

image.altText: Police and ambulance parked near a jetty at a bay

items.title: Marine life bounces back in Swan River after reefs restored

children.content: Work is underway to restore Perth's Swan River and help its severely degraded riverbed, where 1 million tonnes of limestone was taken in the 1990s.

gallery.altText: Man in black puffer with bushland in the background
An excavator on a boat shovelling limestone bricks into the Swan River.
Anemone in the new reef.

items.title: ASX down after Wall Street losses, AUD also lower, oil rises

children.content: The ASX 200 has opened the new trading week with a fall of 1.45 per cent, continuing its downward trend during the month of May. Meanwhile, uncertainty over the Iran war has seen Brent crude oil rise above $110 a barrel. Follow live.

image.altText: A woman is seen in the reflection of a window looking at shares' performance on a board of the ASX

items.title: Hindley climbs into podium contention, Giro 'not over' despite Vingegaard win

children.content: Jai Hindley insists that the Giro d'Italia is not over despite Jonas Vinegagaard proving his mountain dominance with another impressive victory on stage nine.

image.altText: Jai Hindley rides across the line

items.title: Tourism operators priced out of own industry conference

children.content: Struggling businesses in Carnarvon, reliant on tourism, say they want more support to be involved with the 2026 WA Tourism Conference that the region is hosting.

items.title: A Canberra woman's husband said not to book return flights. It was a case of exit trafficking

children.content: Advocates for migrant women say they are seeing more cases of exit trafficking — when an offender uses coercion, threats or deception to organise a person's departure from Australia.

image.altText: A clip art image of a woman, passport and cancelled stamp

items.title: Teenage pedestrian dies after being hit in Perth car park

children.content: A 19-year-old driver is being questioned by police after a teenager is struck by a car in a Perth shopping centre car park and dies in hospital.

image.altText: A car being towed away in a car park

items.title: Harley heroics lift Eagles to stunning upset win over Giants as Dockers extend streak

children.content: West Coast surges to a third victory of the season, defeating GWS by 17 points at Perth Stadium, after Fremantle posts a club record-equalling ninth straight win, beating Essendon by 43 points at the MCG.

items.title: 'Boomer bashing': Are cuts to private health rebates addressing generational or wealth inequality?

children.content: The federal government plans to reduce private health insurance rebates for older Australians, a move one advocate fears could "tip many over the edge".

image.altText: An older woman in a grey jumper

items.title: Why the US, UK and NZ haven't raised interest rates in years

children.content: As Australians pay more on their mortgages, some countries have had no rise in their interest rate in recent years. So what's going on?

items.title: Horror MotoGP crash leaves rider with broken neck

children.content: Alex Márquez is lucky to be alive after he spectacularly wrecked his MotoGP bike during the Catalan Grand Prix in Barcelona, while Johann Zarco was also taken to hospital after an equally terrifying crash later in the race.

items.title: Mass showers and squalor: What spurred ex-Labor minister's NDIS fight

children.content: A former NSW Labor powerbroker and state disability minister pivotal in advocating for the establishment of the NDIS has warned against rushing through sweeping changes to the scheme.

items.title: Cakes and connection as cafe project gives women confidence boost

children.content: Beyond the Kitchen, a course run by Multicultural Services Centre of WA, gives women from migrant, refugee and asylum seeker backgrounds a safe space to develop friendships and hospitality business skills.

items.title: Reasons it's so hard to end a long-term relationship, even when you want to

children.content: It's not just practical and emotional reasons that get in the way of executing a break-up. There are many ways your brain is making you want to stay.

items.title: What to know about the current Ebola outbreak in Uganda and the Congo

children.content: The WHO has declared a global health emergency after more than 80 cases of Ebola were confirmed across two African countries. Here's why the organisation is calling for preparedness, not panic.

items.title: WA's first pair of fainting goats settle into new home

children.content: The goats, which "faint" when stressed or excited, have travelled to a farm near Busselton, where they will be bred as pets.
